title: iberis linifolia subsp. ciliata
intro: Iberis linifolia subsp. ciliata, also known as Alpine Rockcress, is a plant species within the Iberis genus, characterized by its small white flowers and narrow leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Alpine Rockcress include its potential to reduce inflammation, alleviate respiratory issues, and exhibit antioxidant properties. Alpine Rockcress is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support respiratory health and provide relief from colds and flu.
